chunk_summary: Therapeutic goals for uncomplicated moderate wasting focus on recovery, prevention of progression to severe wasting, and risk-stratified supplementation, prioritizing nutrient-dense home diets and counselling per WHO guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand therapeutic goals for moderate acute malnutrition (MAM) in children under 5 years.
Learn WHO recommendations for nutrient-dense home diets and risk-stratified supplementation.
Identify indications for specially formulated supplements in routine and humanitarian settings.
Explore energy requirements and supplementation strategies for recovery in MAM.
key_facts: WHO prioritizes counselling and nutrient-dense home diets for uncomplicated MAM.
Supplementation is recommended for high-risk children (e.g., MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, age <24 months, relapse, comorbidity).
In humanitarian contexts, supplementation may be considered for all children 6–59 months with moderate wasting.
Lipid-based nutrient supplements (LNS) are conditionally preferred by WHO; fortified blended foods are secondary options.
Supplements should contribute 40–60% of daily energy needs, with total energy needs estimated at 100–130 kcal/kg/day.
reference_quote: WHO anchors moderate wasting care in a nutrient-dense diet plus identification and treatment of medical and psychosocial drivers.
